TechCrunch.title: Wikipedia cracks down on the use of AI in article writing
TechCrunch.summary: The site, whose policies are subject to change, has struggled with the issue of AI-generated writing.

TechCrunch.title: Mastodon is making its decentralized social network easier to use with its latest revamp
TechCrunch.summary: Mastodon is redesigning user profiles to simplify the experience and better appeal to mainstream users and organizations.

TechCrunch.title: Netflix confirms it’s raising prices again
TechCrunch.summary: The standard plan without ads now costs $19.99 per month, a $2 increase from the previous $17.99 subscription fee, while the premium plan is also going up by $2 and will now cost $26.99 per month.

TechCrunch.title: 16 of the most interesting startups from YC W26 Demo Day
TechCrunch.summary: From redirecting doomscrolling to training humanoid robots, these 16 startups stood out as the most interesting ones from an overflowing YC cohort.

TechCrunch.title: Google Translate’s real-time headphone translations feature expands to iOS and more countries
TechCrunch.summary: The real-time headphone translations experience keeps each speaker’s tone, emphasis, and cadence intact, so it’s easier to follow the conversation and tell who’s saying what.

TechCrunch.title: OpenAI abandons yet another side quest: ChatGPT’s erotic mode
TechCrunch.summary: It's only the latest of several side projects that the AI startup has ditched over the past week.

TechCrunch.title: Data centers get ready — the Senate wants to see your power bills
TechCrunch.summary: Senators Josh Hawley and Elizabeth Warren want the Energy Information Administration to gather more details about how data centers use power — and how that affects the grid.

TechCrunch.title: Defense startup Shield AI lands $12.7B valuation, up 140%, after US Air Force deal
TechCrunch.summary: In one year, Shield AI's value has leaped 140%. This after it won a contract to be the software provider to Anduril's Fury fighter jet for the U.S. Air Force.

TechCrunch.title: Apple made strides with iOS 26 security, but leaked hacking tools still leave millions exposed to spyware attacks
TechCrunch.summary: Leaked hacking tools threaten the security of millions of older iPhones. Cybersecurity experts weigh in.

TechCrunch.title: Google is launching Search Live globally
TechCrunch.summary: Search Live allows users to point their phone camera at objects to get real-time assistance, enabling back-and-forth conversations that draw on the visual context from the camera feed.

TechCrunch.title: Silicon Valley’s two biggest dramas have intersected: LiteLLM and Delve

TechCrunch.title: ByteDance’s new AI video generation model, Dreamina Seedance 2.0, comes to CapCut
TechCrunch.summary: The new model in CapCut will have built-in protections for making video from real faces or unauthorized intellectual property.

TechCrunch.title: Why hiring the weirdos works

TechCrunch.title: A major hacking tool has leaked online, putting millions of iPhones at risk. Here’s what you need to know.

TechCrunch.title: Cohere launches an open source voice model specifically for transcription
